title: Yosys crashes on an Internal cell import error when implementing a shift under Verific
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

When using the SymbioticEDA Suite, release 2019106A, Yosys crashes when processing the following Verilog.

```verilog
module	mcve(i_a, i_b, o_r);
	input	wire	[31:0]	i_a;
	input	wire	[4:0]	i_b;
	output	wire	[32:0]	o_r;

	wire	signed	[32:0]	w_pre_asr_input, w_pre_asr_shifted;

	assign	w_pre_asr_input = { i_a, 1'b0 };
	assign	w_pre_asr_shifted = w_pre_asr_input >>> i_b;

	assign 	o_r = w_pre_asr_shifted;
endmodule
```

Yosys then crashes when run with,

```
yosys -p 'read -sv mcve.v; prep -top mcve'
```

## Expected behavior

I expect it to prepare the design for formal verification without problems.  (These commands were originally part of an SBY file ...)

## Actual behavior

When running, I get the following error,

```text
ERROR: Found error in internal cell \mcve.$verific$shift_right_4$mcve.v:9$4 ($sshr) at kernel/rtlil.cc:790:
  attribute \src "mcve.v:9"
  cell $sshr $verific$shift_right_4$mcve.v:9$4
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 33
    parameter \B_WIDTH 5
    parameter \A_WIDTH 33
    parameter \B_SIGNED 1
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    connect \Y \w_pre_asr_shifted
    connect \B \i_b
    connect \A \w_pre_asr_input
  end
```

When run under SymbiYosys, the proof fails with an ERROR.  There are no problems when running under the non-commercial version.
code_blocks:
ERROR: Found error in internal cell \mcve.$verific$shift_right_4$mcve.v:9$4 ($sshr) at kernel/rtlil.cc:790:
  attribute \src "mcve.v:9"
  cell $sshr $verific$shift_right_4$mcve.v:9$4
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 33
    parameter \B_WIDTH 5
    parameter \A_WIDTH 33
    parameter \B_SIGNED 1
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    connect \Y \w_pre_asr_shifted
    connect \B \i_b
    connect \A \w_pre_asr_input
  end
